message:
docs(fp-groupby): add crate-level rustdoc (br-wa26 / fd90.68)

Continuation of fd90.61-67 doc-gap audit. fp-groupby is the
standalone groupby aggregation engine - the per-aggregation
kernels powering Series.groupby(...).sum() / .mean() / .std() /
etc. on top of fp-frame's groupby facade. The docs.rs landing
page rendered empty.

Add a //! module doc covering:
  - Why a separate crate: kernel logic + bumpalo-backed
    intermediates separated for isolated test/fuzz/optimization
    without the full fp-frame surface in scope.
  - Aggregation kernels organized by family: sum-family
    (groupby_sum / groupby_prod), central tendency (mean /
    median), variability (std / var), extremes (min / max /
    first / last), counting (count / size / nunique). Plus
    groupby_agg dispatcher with AggFunc enum tag.
  - Tunables: GroupByOptions (sort / skipna / observed-cat),
    GroupByExecutionOptions (arena reuse / hash seed).
  - Approximate primitives: HyperLogLog cardinality estimator
    (used internally by groupby_nunique, exposed for callers).
  - Error reporting: GroupByError.
  - Cross-crate relationships with fp-columnar, fp-frame,
    fp-index, fp-runtime, fp-types.

Pure documentation, no code change. cargo doc -p fp-groupby
--no-deps builds cleanly under RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D warnings'.
65 tests pass; clippy -D warnings clean; ubs Critical=0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-w43q","title":"fd90.21: SqlConnection::table_schema for backend-agnostic column introspection","description":"Add SqlConnection::table_schema(table, schema) -> Result<Option<SqlTableSchema>, IoError> trait method for backend-agnostic column metadata introspection. Default impl returns Ok(None) (backends that can't introspect). rusqlite override uses PRAGMA table_info(table) and maps cid/name/type/notnull/dflt_value/pk to SqlColumnSchema. Schema arg silently ignored on SQLite. Free-fn sql_table_schema(conn, table, schema) wrapper exposes the API. Tests cover unknown table -> None, simple table, table with PK + nullable + default, and multi-schema backend stub.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-25T20:25:26.392204953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T20:29:33.592867620Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T20:29:33.590297206Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fd90","review","sql"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-w4uu","title":"[MEDIUM] parity: MultiIndex .loc multi-axis slicing semantics","description":"Parity-gap audit: MultiIndex `.loc` multi-axis slicing.

**Scope.** pandas `df.loc[(level_0_key, level_1_key), columns]` indexes a row-MultiIndex DataFrame with a tuple. Supports:
- Exact tuple match: `df.loc[('A', 1)]` → Series.
- Partial tuple: `df.loc['A']` → sub-DataFrame (only outer-level filter).
- Slice in tuple: `df.loc[(slice(None), 1)]` → cross-section on inner level.
- pd.IndexSlice builder: `df.loc[pd.IndexSlice[:, 1], :]`.
- Slicing: `df.loc['A':'B', :]` requires sorted MultiIndex; raises `KeyError` otherwise.

**FrankenPandas state.** fp-index exposes `MultiIndex` struct and `DataFrame::xs` / `xs_level`. Check:

```
grep -n "pub fn loc\b\|loc_multi\|xs\|IndexSlice" crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs
grep -n "pub fn " crates/fp-index/src/lib.rs | head -20
```

DISC-006 documents scaffolded row-MultiIndex status. This bead is the concrete audit of `.loc` tuple semantics.

**Severity.** MEDIUM — MultiIndex is claimed in the README capability table but .loc edge cases are not proven at parity.

**Fix shape.**
1. Live-oracle tests in crates/fp-conformance/src/tests/conformance_multiindex_loc.rs covering 10 inputs: exact tuple match, partial tuple (outer), partial tuple (inner via slice), sorted-range slice, unsorted-range slice (expect error), pd.IndexSlice form, missing key KeyError, duplicate MultiIndex labels, NaN level values, empty frame.
2. Any divergence → child [HIGH] bead per failure case.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T02:11:52.107787752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T03:30:55.891849103Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T03:30:55.889372396Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["loc","multiindex","parity-gap","review"]}

Public surface: GroupByOptions / GroupByExecutionOptions, GroupByError, AggFunc enum, groupby_sum / mean / count / min / max / first / last / std / var / median / nunique / prod / size aggregations, groupby_agg dispatcher, HyperLogLog approximate-distinct-count primitive. Pure docs. Verify cargo doc cleanly under RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:20:27.880362095Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:22:33.296973084Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T06:22:33.294410403Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fd90","review"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-weh1","title":"[LOW] community: no .github/FUNDING.yml — no Sponsor button / funding channels declared","description":"GitHub Sponsors / funding configuration audit.

**Observation.** `ls .github/FUNDING.yml` → No such file. No declared funding channels (GitHub Sponsors / Open Collective / Liberapay / etc.).

**Context.** For an open-source library targeting a sizable user base (pandas has tens of millions of users; frankenpandas targets their Rust-curious subset), a documented funding mechanism is a cheap way to let engaged users contribute financially. GitHub renders a "Sponsor" button on the repo header when `FUNDING.yml` is present; without it, the button is absent and engaged users have no path.

**Severity.** LOW — no operational impact. No release blocker. No code quality issue. Filing truthfully as LOW; user-visible polish, optional feature.

**Fix shape.** `.github/FUNDING.yml`:
```yaml
# Map the repo's "Sponsor" button to one or more funding platforms.
# Each line enables a platform. Remove entries that don't apply.

github: [<org-or-user>]           # GitHub Sponsors
# open_collective: frankenpandas
# liberapay: <name>
# ko_fi: <name>
# custom: ["https://frankenpandas.dev/sponsor"]
```

Requires a decision: does the maintainer have an active GitHub Sponsors account? If yes, one-line file. If not, skip this bead entirely.

Blast radius: 1 tiny file (< 10 lines), zero code changes.

Prerequisites: Maintainer decides which funding channel(s) to expose. Nothing else.

Related:
- br-8k1i (SECURITY.md), br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) — same .github/ polish tier.
- No interlocks with other beads.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:18:55.853913193Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:58.138001Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:58.138001Z","close_reason":".github/FUNDING.yml already shipped on main.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["community","funding","review"]}

@@ -1,5 +1,66 @@
 #![forbid(unsafe_code)]
 
+//! Standalone groupby aggregation engine for **frankenpandas** —
+//! the per-aggregation kernels that power `Series.groupby(...).sum()`
+//! / `.mean()` / `.std()` / etc. on top of fp-frame's groupby
+//! façade.
+//!
+//! ## Why a separate crate
+//!
+//! fp-frame's `SeriesGroupBy` / DataFrame groupby wrappers expose
+//! the user-facing API. The actual hash-build, group-key materialise,
+//! per-key reduction, and bumpalo-backed intermediate arena live
+//! here — separated so per-agg kernels can be tested, fuzzed, and
+//! optimized in isolation without the full fp-frame surface in
+//! scope.
+//!
+//! ## Aggregation kernels (pandas `.groupby(...).<agg>()`)
+//!
+//! - **Sum-family**: [`groupby_sum`] / [`groupby_sum_with_options`],
+//!   [`groupby_prod`].
+//! - **Central tendency**: [`groupby_mean`], [`groupby_median`].
+//! - **Variability**: [`groupby_std`], [`groupby_var`].
+//! - **Extremes**: [`groupby_min`], [`groupby_max`],
+//!   [`groupby_first`], [`groupby_last`].
+//! - **Counting**: [`groupby_count`], [`groupby_size`],
+//!   [`groupby_nunique`].
+//! - **Dispatcher**: [`groupby_agg`] takes an [`AggFunc`] enum tag
+//!   and dispatches to the matching kernel — useful for callers
+//!   that want a uniform entry point (e.g. `df.groupby(k).agg(['sum',
+//!   'mean'])` style multi-agg).
+//!
+//! ## Tunables
+//!
+//! - [`GroupByOptions`]: per-call shape options (sort group keys,
+//!   skipna policy, observed-categorical, ...).
+//! - [`GroupByExecutionOptions`]: lower-level execution knobs
+//!   (bumpalo arena reuse hints, hash-build seed, ...).
+//!
+//! ## Approximate primitives
+//!
+//! - [`HyperLogLog`]: cardinality-estimation sketch. Used internally
+//!   by [`groupby_nunique`] when the underlying group has a large
+//!   number of unique values; exposed publicly for callers building
+//!   their own approximate aggregations.
+//!
+//! ## Error reporting
+//!
+//! [`GroupByError`] enumerates failure modes (key column dtype
+//! mismatch, length mismatch, alignment-plan violation, ...).
+//!
+//! ## Cross-crate relationships
+//!
+//! - **fp-columnar** ([`Column`], [`ColumnError`]) for column ops.
+//! - **fp-frame** ([`FrameError`], [`Series`]) for the wrapper
+//!   types that expose this crate's kernels via `.groupby()`.
+//! - **fp-index** ([`Index`], [`IndexError`], [`IndexLabel`],
+//!   [`align_union`], [`validate_alignment_plan`]) for the
+//!   group-key index alignment.
+//! - **fp-runtime** ([`EvidenceLedger`], [`RuntimePolicy`]) for
+//!   optional decision recording.
+//! - **fp-types** ([`Scalar`], [`NullKind`], [`Timedelta`]) for
+//!   the underlying value machinery.
+
 use std::{cmp::Ordering, collections::HashMap, mem::size_of};
 
 use bumpalo::{Bump, collections::Vec as BumpVec};
